---
title: "Introduction"
---

Welcome to the first chapter. Below is a standard, static Python cell. It is executed purely at render-time, and its outputs are frozen into the resulting page:

```{python}
print("This is a standard cell. It runs when the book is rendered by Quarto.")
```

Below is an interactive `quarto-live` Pyodide cell. You can edit and run it right in your browser!

```{pyodide}
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Create some data for plotting
data = {'x': [1, 2, 3, 4, 5],
        'y': [2, 4, 1, 8, 7]}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)

plt.plot(df['x'], df['y'], marker='o', linestyle='-', color='b')
plt.title('Sample Interactive Plot')
plt.xlabel('X-axis')
plt.ylabel('Y-axis')
plt.show()
```
